preamble: The elision of the vowel [a] is a common process of sound loss. Depending on its position in the word, it is classified as apheresis (initial), syncope (medial), or apocope (final).

examples.note: Syncope: loss of medial unstressed vowel.
Apocope: loss or extreme reduction of word-final vowels.

sources: Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction. MIT Press.
Lass, R. (1997). Historical Linguistics and Language Change. Cambridge University Press.
